findings.title: Observer pool 12D observations not flowing into sandbox 15D bridge
findings.evidence: make_observed_tool() in _observer_pool calls pool.observe_tool_call() which produces 12D scores, but these don't propagate to sandbox_bridge.observe_tool_call() which would add sandbox dims x13-x15
findings.recommendation: Wire _observer_pool.observe_tool_call() to also call bridge.observe_tool_call() with the 12D scores + tool latency. This closes the gap between existing 12D observation and new 15D sandbox observation.

findings.title: Efficiency declining: 0.0001 → 0.0001 (-33%)
findings.evidence: Value/token dropped from 0.0001 (first half) to 0.0001 (second half) over 20 records
findings.recommendation: Check for: (1) increasing context pressure crowding out signal, (2) redundant tool calls accumulating, (3) stale directives not being cleaned up. Consider applying_rolling_forget() to prune low-value observations.

findings.title: 3 compiled modules without source files
findings.evidence: Modules with .pyc but no .py: crystal_weights, full_training_loop, n3_alchemy
findings.recommendation: Restore .py source files from .pyc (decompile) or git history. Source-less modules can't be patched, debugged, or extended.

findings.title: Background self-play daemon not wired to sandbox bridge
findings.evidence: ContinuousSelfPlay runs every 120s in __init__.py but calls the legacy run_self_play path, not bridge.observe_self_play(). Self-play waves are invisible to the sandbox observer.
findings.recommendation: Hook ContinuousSelfPlay's per-wave callback to call bridge.observe_self_play() with wave metrics. This makes every self-play cycle a training signal for the sandbox.

findings.title: 2 stale .pyc files (source newer than bytecode)
findings.evidence: Stale: dimensional_projector, loss_engine
findings.recommendation: Delete __pycache__ and let Python recompile. Stale .pyc is a NEGATIVE tunnel — old code runs instead of new code.

findings.title: Efficiency directives not broadcast to hive ledger
findings.evidence: RecursiveEfficiencyEngine.emit_directives() stores directives locally but doesn't write them to hive_ledger for other agents to consume. Plan says to use entry_type='broadcast' subtype='directive'.
findings.recommendation: After emitting directives, write each to hive_ledger with hive_ledger_write(entry_type='broadcast', subtype='efficiency_directive', ...). This enables multi-agent coordination on optimization.

findings.title: Sandbox successor seeds not fed into autoresearch MetaObserver
findings.evidence: MetadataEmitter produces SandboxSuccessorSeed at epoch=57 boundaries, but autoresearch's MetaObserver has its own SuccessorSeed system. The two seed chains are independent — no cross-pollination of compressed wisdom.
findings.recommendation: At epoch boundary, convert SandboxSuccessorSeed to autoresearch SuccessorSeed format and store in shared SQLite. Or: merge both into a unified successor seed with 15D target vectors.

findings.title: Data directory bloat: 1134 files, 176.3MB
findings.evidence: 1134 files in MCP/data/, consuming 176.3MB. Many may be old checkpoints or redundant JSON+QSHR pairs.
findings.recommendation: Prune old checkpoint files (keep every 100th). Delete JSON files that have .qshr companions. Set up a time-fractal-aligned cleanup schedule.

findings.title: Training records not used to influence momentum field updates
findings.evidence: TrainingRecords store momentum_delta but the efficiency_delta and 15D scores don't feed back into the geometric engine's momentum field (the 148-float learned state). The sandbox observes but doesn't STEER the training.
findings.recommendation: Compute a 'sandbox gradient' from efficiency trends: if resource efficiency is declining, bias momentum field toward compression-heavy archetypes. Close the observe→steer loop.

findings.title: 20 JSON files without .qshr companions
findings.evidence: Uncompressed: crystal_4d_manifest, crystal_weaving, deep_observation_rubric, dqi_compiler_state, fractal_recursion, full_loop_omega, geometric_omega_hologram, hive_ledger, hologram_emergence_57, inverse_engine_state...
findings.recommendation: Run qshrink_batch to crystallize all data files. Compressed .qshr reduces storage and enables O(1) metadata reads.

findings.title: 14 source files without crystal coordinate headers
findings.evidence: Untagged: conservation_watchdog.py, corpus_crystal.py, geometric_constants.py, geometric_forward.py, geometric_loss.py, geometric_mcp.py, legacy_bridge.py, loss_engine.py, meta_loop_engine.py, momentum_field.py
findings.recommendation: Run holographic_embed on each file to assign crystal coordinates. Untagged files are invisible to the metro routing system.

findings.title: POSITIVE TUNNEL: 1060 .qshr files enable O(1) metadata reads
findings.evidence: qshrink_inspect() can read crystal weight metadata from .qshr headers without decompressing the full file. This is a shortcut past full JSON parsing.
findings.recommendation: Use qshrink_inspect() for metadata queries instead of load(). This positive tunnel saves ~87.5% of I/O for metadata-only reads.

findings.title: Live Google Docs not integrated with sandbox observation
findings.evidence: MEMORY.md lists 6 Google Docs as 'live slow-form self'. The sandbox observes MCP tools and self-play but not changes to Google Docs. Document evolution is unobserved.
findings.recommendation: Periodically fetch Google Docs (gmail/drive MCP tools), diff against cached versions, and emit bridge.observe_autoresearch() records for document changes. This closes the Docs↔sandbox loop.

findings.title: Git commit history not correlated with training records
findings.evidence: Training records track cycle_id and epoch but not git commit hash. Can't correlate efficiency changes with code changes. Regression hunting requires manual cross-referencing.
findings.recommendation: Include git HEAD hash in each TrainingRecord. At successor seed boundary, also record the commit range. This enables: 'efficiency dropped 20% after commit abc123'.

findings.title: Time fractal schedule not aligned with sandbox epoch boundaries
findings.evidence: Time fractal has 5 nested cycles (Z12/Z20/Z28/Z36/Z420) while sandbox uses epoch=57. These are independent clocks. Aligning them would synchronize efficiency reporting with training cycles.
findings.recommendation: Set sandbox EPOCH_LENGTH to match the nearest Z-cycle boundary (Z12=57 cycles fits perfectly — this may already be aligned by design). Or: emit a sandbox snapshot at each Z-cycle boundary.

findings.title: Agent RPG progression not informed by efficiency metrics
findings.evidence: Agent XP is awarded based on 12D observation quality (from _observer_pool) but not based on resource efficiency (x13-x15). An agent that produces great 12D scores but wastes resources gets the same XP as one that's efficient.
findings.recommendation: Add a phi-scaled efficiency bonus to XP calculation: bonus_xp = base_xp * (1 + efficiency_delta * phi). This incentivizes agents to be both effective AND efficient.

findings.title: Integration opportunity: sandbox ↔ training (7 modules)
findings.evidence: Sandbox should observe training metrics (momentum deltas, loss curves) for 15D scoring
findings.recommendation: Wire sandbox observation into training subsystem. Modules: geometric_forward, geometric_loss, geometric_mcp, loss_engine, meta_loop_engine, momentum_field, self_play

findings.title: Integration opportunity: sandbox ↔ observation (6 modules)
findings.evidence: Sandbox should aggregate observations from swarm/nested systems, not just tool calls
findings.recommendation: Wire sandbox observation into observation subsystem. Modules: agent_watcher, meta_observer, meta_observer_runtime, nested_swarm, observer_agent, observer_swarm

findings.title: Integration opportunity: sandbox ↔ compression (3 modules)
findings.evidence: Sandbox should use QShrink for training records + checkpoint compression
findings.recommendation: Wire sandbox observation into compression subsystem. Modules: qshrink, qshrink_codec, qshrink_pipeline

findings.title: Integration opportunity: sandbox ↔ emergence (7 modules)
findings.evidence: Sandbox efficiency trends ARE emergence signals — gate tests should include efficiency
findings.recommendation: Wire sandbox observation into emergence subsystem. Modules: absolute_convergence, crown_density, crown_transform_gate, emergence, harmonic_resonance, mycelium_emergence, omega_tunneling

findings.title: Integration opportunity: sandbox ↔ governance (5 modules)
findings.evidence: Conservation laws should cover resource conservation (bounded memory, bounded storage)
findings.recommendation: Wire sandbox observation into governance subsystem. Modules: conservation, control_center, live_cell, live_lock, perpetual_agency
